title: Regional economic review trends positive, but sustainability unclear
description: The Midwestern economy took positive steps last month, but analysts don’t yet have a read on the sustainability of this leap.

title: 'Ghosts of Fourth Street' explores the promise and perils of childhood memories
description: Author Laurie Hertzel grew up in a house crowded with siblings, stories, and the occasional ghost. She reflects on the stories that bind families together and stories that tear them apart.

title: Man sentenced in 2025 Yankton homicide
description: A Yankton man is sentenced to life in prison in connection to a 2025 homicide.

title: Scaffolding up in state Capitol building as rotunda restoration begins
description: Renovations and restorations at the state Capitol building in Pierre are underway. Updates to the rotunda are happening for the next six months.

title: Analysis: School lunch legislation proves persistence pays in Pierre
description: How did a Democrat from Sioux Falls manage to get a bill to support school lunches over the finish line? South Dakota Searchlight Editor-in-Chief Seth Tupper explains the role of compromise in state politics.

title: Lake Mitchell project hinges on state board approval before fall start
description: After a late-2025 hiccup","target":"NEW","attachSourceUrl":false,"url":"https://www.sdpb.org/environment/2025-09-17/lake-mitchell-restoration-pushed-back-to-fall-2026","_id":"0000019d-4aa6-d63c-a3bd-ffef6ca50000","_type":"ff658216-e70f-39d0-b660-bdfe57a5599a"},"_id":"0000019d-4aa6-d63c-a3bd-ffef6ca50001","_type":"809caec9-30e2-3666-8b71-b32ddbffc288"}">a late-2025 hiccup, the Lake Mitchell restoration project","target":"NEW","attachSourceUrl":false,"url":"https://www.cityofmitchellsd.gov/DocumentCenter/View/2721/Lake-Mitchell-Master-Plan","_id":"0000019d-4aa6-d18f-abbf-ebefe9800000","_type":"ff658216-e70f-39d0-b660-bdfe57a5599a"},"_id":"0000019d-4aa6-d18f-abbf-ebefe9800001","_type":"809caec9-30e2-3666-8b71-b32ddbffc288"}">Lake Mitchell restoration project is on course to begin this year. Though, it’s contingent on a state board’s approval.

title: Holiday travel could see weather complications
description: Easter travel plans could be complicated by a large weather system moving through the state. The most heavily impacted portion will likely be East River and the Glacial Lakes.

title: SD Arts Council offering America 250 Community Arts Grants
description: The South Dakota arts council is putting out the invitation to apply for America 250 Community Arts Grants.

title: Two months from primary, new South Dakota voters prepare to prove citizenship
description: People whose names do not match their birth certificates may face administrative challenges when registering to vote. Sara Lampert, Ph.D., explains how new rules impact married women and transgender people.

title: Ribbon cutting in Brookings shines light on gubernatorial campaign issue
description: A ribbon cutting in Brookings for a facility expansion gets at the heart of one of the looming campaign questions this year.

title: Rep. Johnson says USPS working on issues, more to be done
description: South Dakota’s lone U.S. House of Representatives member says there’s progress at the local USPS.

title: State announces new round of grants from opioid settlement funds
description: Young people, former inmates and homeless individuals are some of the groups the state is targeting with its latest round of opioid settlement dollars.

title: Hansen Outlines Vision for South Dakota’s Economic Future | In The Moment
description: Republican gubernatorial candidate and Speaker of the House Jon Hansen kicked off his campaign as the first in the field. In a July 2025 appearance on In The Moment, he emphasized his commitment to state freedom and economic growth.

title: Rep. Johnson says USPS working on issues, more to be done
description: South Dakota’s lone U.S. House of Representatives member says there’s progress at the local USPS.

title: Republican Gubernatorial Candidate Toby Doeden Outlines Economic Vision | In The Moment
description: Republican gubernatorial candidate Toby Doeden spoke with In The Moment in July 2025 about his vision for the state’s economic growth.

title: Artemis II astronauts have a message for Earth. Will political leaders listen?
description: The Artemis II astronauts have shared messages of unity and beauty from their vantage point in space. The Dakota Political Junkies consider what this moment might mean for political discourse.

title: South Dakota CDL holders must update medical certification before July deadline
description: New regulations are requiring most CDL holders to update their license with a medical certification status.

title: Smithsonian selects All My Relatives Festival for nationwide festival series
description: A Sioux Falls-based Indigenous festival is selected to take part in a nationwide program marking the 250<sup>th</sup> anniversary of the United States.

title: Rep. Dusty Johnson Outlines Gubernatorial Vision, Focuses on Fiscal Discipline | In The Moment
description: In July 2025, U.S. Representative and Republican gubernatorial candidate Dusty Johnson visited the SDPB studio to reflect on his political career and discuss how he plans to apply his budget-conscious mentality to the governor's office.

title: Rhoden announces $5M South Dakota Defense Institute
description: Gov. Larry Rhoden, in his State of the State address, set his sights on defense as the next tentpole of the state economy. Now, he says a new development will make that a reality.

title: School districts prepare for downstream impacts of legislative year
description: In the postmortem of the legislative session, the implications of policy decisions are beginning to trickle down to communities. For school districts, it means looking at what changes will be in store for the upcoming academic year.

title: Rapid City Schools target first successful bond vote in over 50 years
description: Rapid City Area Schools have advanced a bond issue for voters to consider. If passed, it would mark the first successful bond vote in the community since 1973.

title: South Dakota's contribution to national time capsule unveiled
description: South Dakota’s contribution to a “America’s Time Capsule” celebrating the nation’s 250<sup>th</sup> anniversary is set.

title: SDPB News/SD News Watch GOP gubernatorial debate airs live Monday night
description: Four Republican candidates for South Dakota governor are squaring off in a debate Monday night hosted by SDPB and South Dakota News Watch.

title: President approves disaster declaration for destructive December windstorm
description: The White House is approving South Dakota’s disaster declaration for a major winter windstorm in the Black Hills.

title: Urban chickens one vote away from nesting in Rapid City
description: The Rapid City Council is giving residents something to cluck about with tentative approval of a proposal allowing backyard chickens. It’s now one vote away from passage.

title: Clear contrasts in Republican taxation policy highlighted in debate
description: Taxation drew some of the sharpest contrasts in the SDPB/South Dakota New watch Republican primary debate.

title: 2026 Republican Gubernatorial Candidate Debate
byline: By SDPB Staff, SDPB News Staff
description: SDPB News presents the 2026 South Dakota Republican Gubernatorial Candidate Debate.

title: Republicans distinguish themselves in latest debate
description: Four Republican candidates for governor sought to separate themselves from each other in order to woo primary voters. SDPB's Dakota Political Junkies ask: Were any of them successful?

title: SDPB Republican gubernatorial debate offers insight into priorities before primaries
description: With several candidates vying for governor, each brings their own working philosophy to the table. SDPB’s Republican primary debate dug into their plans of attack on several key policy issues.

title: National Weather Service encourages severe weather preparation this week
description: It’s South Dakota’s Weather Awareness Week, an annual reminder from the National Weather Service encouraging people to stay vigilant and prepare now for severe weather.

title: Despite some reservations, state investment officer receives recommended increase
description: Despite reservations from some lawmakers, the state’s investment officer is receiving a salary increase in line with other state employees, as well as an incentive bonus.

title: Dan Ahlers says 50 years of Republican leadership proves it's time to send a Democrat to the governor's mansion
description: Dan Ahlers is executive director for South Dakota's Democratic Party. He's running for governor. He says fair taxes, investments in education, and affordability are three key areas of concern for voters.

title: School district finances, taxes, AI and data centers: Legislators decide memo topics
description: Lawmakers are asking state researchers to look into various topics and report back with their findings. These memos sometimes turn into the foundation for policy and new laws.

title: Gov. Larry Rhoden outlines policy priorities as early voting approaches in South Dakota
description: Early voting in South Dakota begins on April 17. Gov. Larry Rhoden makes his case to voters for national defense and gun-related industry.

title: Officials seeking public comment on proposed Powertech uranium project
description: The Bureau of Land Management is asking for public comments about the impact of a proposed uranium on public lands in southwest South Dakota.

title: Parks Service seek volunteers for Missouri River cleanup near Yankton
description: A cleanup effort of the Missouri River near Yankton is nearing its twentieth year of action.

title: Drought mandates fire restrictions across Black Hills until December
description: Long-term drought conditions are impacting the Black Hills on both sides of the border, and the complications are compounded by a lack of spring moisture so far.

title: Emily Bright at Augustana University for National Poetry Month
description: Minnesota poet Emily Bright talks about the influence of Mary Oliver and the need to respond to difficult historical and cultural events in her poems.

title: Rhoden says demand justifies $4M taxpayer investment in firearms program
description: The governor is using the taxpayer-backed Future Fund to make a $4 million investment in a gunsmithing program at Western Dakota Tech in Rapid City.

title: Testifiers say Missouri River water needed for 'national security' at Ellsworth
description: A U.S. House committee hears testimony on a bill ordering a study done","target":"NEW","attachSourceUrl":false,"url":"https://www.congress.gov/119/bills/hr7288/BILLS-119hr7288ih.pdf","_id":"0000019d-9802-df45-a5ff-da765dfe0000","_type":"ff658216-e70f-39d0-b660-bdfe57a5599a"},"_id":"0000019d-9802-df45-a5ff-da765dfe0001","_type":"809caec9-30e2-3666-8b71-b32ddbffc288"}">a bill ordering a study done on the feasibility of using Missouri River Water for western South Dakota use.

title: America at 250: What to keep? What not to repeat?
description: The Dakota Conference on the Northern Plains envisions the future of the region. We talk with Christel Gollnick about the "Stone Soup" metaphor of community abundance.

title: Can the 'Mississippi Miracle' framework boost state reading scores?
description: The future of education in South Dakota is a question the state’s Republican gubernatorial hopefuls contended with during the most recent debate.

title: DCI rules troopers justified in shooting, injuring suspect after pursuit
description: A state investigation finds two Highway Patrol troopers were justified in firing their guns during an arrest West River.

title: Poll shows Johnson near crucial threshold in GOP primary race
description: A new polls on the South Dakota Republican gubernatorial primary race shows an increase of support for Congressman Dusty Johnson and state lawmaker Jon Hansen. We talk with South Dakota News Watch reporter Alexander Rifaat about the results.

title: Avera receives largest single grant in system's history
description: Avera receives the largest single gift in the health care system’s history. Avera said the $35 million will support pediatric care “for the next generation.”

title: Backyard coops approved for Rapid City as enthusiasts stress benefits
description: Rapid City leadership has had their say: a ban on backyard chicken coops is for the birds.

title: Federal Medicaid expansion engagement requirements could impact 1,200 South Dakotans
description: South Dakota’s Board of Department of Social Services is still wrapping its arms around the impacts of major federal legislation, in particular changes to Medicaid engagement requirements.

title: Previewing the third and final GOP gubernatorial primary debate
description: Two South Dakota Republican gubernatorial primary debates are in the books, and one more is on the horizon. We get a preview with this week's Dakota Political Junkies.

title: Jackley tells feds communication lacking, points to South Dakota on eminent domain
description: South Dakota’s top legal official spoke on a Congressional roundtable looking at legal challenges facing American agriculture.

title: Artists gather to explore connection between arts and healing
description: The 2026 South Dakota State Arts Conference kicks off this week. Keynote speaker Christopher Bailey shares stories of arts, healing, and the ways we become fully human.

title: New prison groundbreaking comes with call to fund, support rehabilitation
description: South Dakota breaks ground on a new men’s penitentiary in Sioux Falls. However, officials say buildings alone don’t equate to meaningful prison reform.

title: RCAS teacher raises above legislative minimum, below inflation rate
description: Rapid City Area schoolteachers are slated for a 2.5 percent wage increase beginning in FY27. While above the amount allocated by the Legislature, it remains below the rate of inflation that has nagged consumers across the country.

title: Youth trapping program closed for 2026
description: South Dakota’s Youth Trapping Recruitment Program is closed for the season.

title: DOC lacks population-specific programs, otherwise has 'right things' in place
description: South Dakota’s Department of Corrections has the “right” programming, but not the means to implement all its goals. It's also lacking in programming for certain populations, like Native Americans. That’s the latest from a task force aimed at rehabilitation reform and lowering recidivism.

title: Motorcycle training range 'darn good value' for taxpayers, advocates say
description: A new training range in Sioux Falls is something officials are calling a win for taxpayers and roadway safety.

title: South Dakota Symphony premieres Iranian composer's tribute to persistence
description: South Dakota's premiere orchestra heads to a season finale with music from the Middle East, a piano concerto, and a showcase of the orchestra's musicians.

title: Some county auditors still without ballots a week after deadline
description: A delay in absentee ballot deliveries is continuing to impact voters in communities across the state. As of Thursday, several of the county auditors SDPB reached out to have not yet received their 2026 primary ballots.

title: Brookings' Urban Forest Initiative returns
description: The city of Brookings is bringing back its Urban Forest Initiative this year.

title: State Supreme Court rules in favor of officers in 'Marsy's Law' debate
description: The South Dakota Supreme Court unanimously rules that certain crime victim protections extend to law enforcement officers.

title: Rapid City leaders await next round of opioid settlement dollars
description: Pennington County, one of many named in nationwide opioid legal settlements, is now looking to use that windfall wisely. Local leadership heard a presentation on the matter that included the priorities for addiction treatment.

title: Flandreau celebrates new Boys & Girls Club child care center
description: The Clubhouse Early Childhood Enrichment Center is the result of a partnership between the Boys & Girls Club of the Northern Plains, the Flandreau Santee Sioux Tribe, the City of Flandreau, Dakota Layers egg farm and others.

title: Free customer service training sessions across state next week
description: Travel South Dakota and the South Dakota Retailers Association are holding free seminars to customer service businesses.

title: Two local governments violated open meeting laws, commission says
description: A state commission finds two local government entities violated open meetings laws, while asking for more information on two other complaints.

title: SFPD officer injured in shooting; Law enforcement leaders renew calls for reform
description: A shooting that left a Sioux Falls police officer injured is spurring law enforcement to renew the call for the state to take a hard look at its parole and prison system as a whole.

title: SD Supreme Court upholds conviction of man charged with threatening Trump
description: The state Supreme Court is upholding the conviction of a man charged with threatening to kill President Donald Trump at an event in South Dakota.

title: SDSU International Journeys
description: South Dakota State University has just over 770 international students representing 76 countries. In recent years, enrollment has remained relatively consistent, though officials say the number is subject to change.

title: Election observation training available ahead of primary voting
description: Poll watching can offer a chance for citizens to be directly involved in ensuring the security of elections. As this continues to be a hot-button issue in Pierre, the state chapter of the ACLU is hoping to teach South Dakotans how to get involved.

title: DCI: Sioux Falls detectives were justified firing weapons during arrest
description: State investigators are ruling two Sioux Falls police officers were justified in shooting and injuring a suspect during an arrest.

title: 'No doubt client would have died;' Response leads to honors at Care Campus
description: Three employees of the Pennington County Sheriff's Department and the Rapid City Care Campus received a meritorious service award for their role in saving a life.

title: Newly formed committee seeks to overturn increased sales tax for property tax relief law
description: A group, calling itself “South Dakotans for Fair Taxes\"","target":"NEW","attachSourceUrl":false,"url":"https://sdcfr.sdsos.gov/Document.aspx?DocumentID=101605&type=doc","_id":"0000019d-db24-da52-a7fd-ff2e59cd0000","_type":"ff658216-e70f-39d0-b660-bdfe57a5599a"},"_id":"0000019d-db24-da52-a7fd-ff2e59cd0001","_type":"809caec9-30e2-3666-8b71-b32ddbffc288"}">“South Dakotans for Fair Taxes" is launching a referred law campaign. The campaign committee seeks to overturn a new law that uses a scheduled sales tax increase for property tax relief.

title: Crews installing new Sioux Falls SculptureWalk exhibits this weekend
description: SculptureWalk features roughly 80 pieces of art sprinkled throughout downtown Sioux Falls.

title: Rapid City to boost security measures in two public high schools
description: Two Rapid City schools are increasing security measures with metal detectors students will need to walk through before entry. It’s a matter administration says will increase safety for the facilities in an era of threats against schools.

title: Pe' Sla mining battle continues after protest at corporate office
description: A second day of protests erupts in response to a mining operation near Pe’Sla, a Black Hills sacred site. This time, at the headquarters of the mining organization behind the development.

title: Campaign asks voters if scheduled tax increase should go to property tax relief
description: Efforts to refer a Legislature-approved bill","target":"NEW","attachSourceUrl":false,"url":"https://www.sdpb.org/politics/2026-04-29/newly-formed-committee-seeks-to-overturn-increased-sales-tax-for-property-tax-relief-law?_gl=1*g3jior*_gcl_au*MTY4OTk2MDI5MC4xNzcxODU4OTYx*_ga*OTE2NjI4NDg0LjE3NjI0NTY4MTU.*_ga_BWGZXV2BJN*czE3Nzc1ODMxNDAkbzM4MyRnMSR0MTc3NzU4MzE0MyRqNTckbDAkaDA.","_id":"0000019d-e083-d707-a7df-e3c785410000","_type":"ff658216-e70f-39d0-b660-bdfe57a5599a"},"_id":"0000019d-e083-d707-a7df-e3c785410001","_type":"809caec9-30e2-3666-8b71-b32ddbffc288"}">Efforts to refer a Legislature-approved bill are underway. The campaign disapproves of using a scheduled increase in sales taxes for property tax relief.

title: Activists seek to disrupt mining operation near sacred Black Hills site
description: Opponents of a Black Hills mining operation have sued to halt it and are now acting on the scene. Among the complaints is location, considered a sacred site in Lakota culture.

title: RCFD may need to reduce ambulance coverage area without funding solutions
description: The Rapid City Fire Department estimates it serves around 150,000 people in Rapid City and the surrounding metro area, but many could lose ambulance coverage outside city limits unless funding agreements are made.

title: Report: South Dakota's average gas prices surpasses $4 per gallon
description: The latest data from GasBuddy shows  the state’s average is $4.04 a gallon, up 33 cents from last week

title: South Dakota receives $10 million in Purdue Pharma settlement
description: South Dakota is receiving $10 million dollars as part of a settlement with a major pharmaceutical company for its role in fueling the opioid crisis.

title: Referred law campaign could delay budget transfer, affect other legislation
description: Nearly $56 million in South Dakota’s state budget could be in limbo if a referred law campaign gets on the ballot. If the campaign were to succeed, state lawmakers would also have to address other legislation passed this year.

title: Appeals court upholds Briley Piper's death sentence
description: A federal appeals court is upholding the sentence of the only inmate on South Dakota’s death row.

title: Batcheller: 'Neighborhood Empowerment Labs' will help Sioux Falls residents envision the future of the city
description: Urban planner Joe Batcheller wants to be the next mayor of Sioux Falls. He talks about improving transportation and helping neighborhood organizations create the city they want to live in.

title: Rhoden reinstates rural tourism grant program
description: Travel South Dakota is reinstating a grant program designed to boost tourism-related businesses and organizations in rural communities.

title: South Dakota trust assets valued above $900B despite "chunk of assets" exiting
description: Assets in South Dakota trusts are valued above $900 billion despite a large crypto custodian going elsewhere.

title: Smith: Sioux Falls 'too rich' to not deal with homelessness
description: Jamie Smith wants to be the next mayor of Sioux Falls. He talks about leadership, housing, and how to keep the city a welcoming place for people across the region.

title: Federal court grants two-week mining reprieve at Pe’ Sla
description: The events surrounding Pe’ Sla are still unfolding as a federal court grants a two-week drilling restraining order. That ruling came late Monday night.

title: State approves variance request for new Lake Mitchell Dam
description: A state panel has paved the way forward for a new dam at Lake Mitchell.

title: Governor activates South Dakota Drought Task Force
description: The state Drought Task Force coordinates the exchange of drought-related information among government agencies, officials say.

title: Electronic vehicle titles and elk hunting changes discussed at rules meeting
description: A state legislative committee approved a swath of rule changes at its May 05 meeting","target":"NEW","attachSourceUrl":false,"url":"https://mylrc.sdlegislature.gov/api/Documents/306745.pdf","_id":"0000019d-f983-dc23-a7dd-f9d7d0e60000","_type":"ff658216-e70f-39d0-b660-bdfe57a5599a"},"_id":"0000019d-f983-dc23-a7dd-f9d7d0e60001","_type":"809caec9-30e2-3666-8b71-b32ddbffc288"}">May 5 meeting. including one that could make buying a new car easier and changes to elk hunting.

title: New state math standards leaves teachers holding breath for changes
description: The governor announced this week a change in standards for state math students. At the same time, educators say there could be some issues with the plan.

title: Operation: Prairie Thunder completes operation in Mitchell
description: The Department of Public Safety released the latest figures","target":"NEW","attachSourceUrl":false,"url":"https://news.sd.gov/news?id=news_kb_article_view&sys_id=1cecebc81bf047547e05ea85624bcbaf","_id":"0000019e-033e-dc23-a7df-6b7e47480000","_type":"ff658216-e70f-39d0-b660-bdfe57a5599a"},"_id":"0000019e-033e-dc23-a7df-6b7e469d0000","_type":"809caec9-30e2-3666-8b71-b32ddbffc288"}">released the latest figures from a public safety campaign. Operation: Prairie Thunder","target":"NEW","attachSourceUrl":false,"url":"https://www.sdpb.org/crime-courts/2025-07-28/rhoden-announces-new-public-safety-initiative-to-reduce-crime","_id":"0000019e-0340-da3d-a59f-e76a5f080000","_type":"ff658216-e70f-39d0-b660-bdfe57a5599a"},"_id":"0000019e-0340-da3d-a59f-e76a5f080001","_type":"809caec9-30e2-3666-8b71-b32ddbffc288"}">Operation: Prairie Thunder led to just under a dozen arrests during a two-day patrol in Mitchell.

title: South Dakota voter registration deadline is May 18
description: Election officials are reminding South Dakotans the deadline to register before the June primaries is approaching.

title: GFP outlines plans to communicate invasive species danger to boaters
description: A state commission heard plans to mitigate the introduction of aquatic invasive species to South Dakota waterways.

title: SURF marks major construction milestone in neutrino research project
description: The Sanford Underground Research or Facility, or SURF, has marked a milestone in the development of its cutting edge physics project.

title: Erickson: Literacy is key to public safety progress in Sioux Falls
description: Christine Erickson wants to be the next mayor of Sioux Falls. She talks about youth diversion programs, literacy initiatives, and 'thoughtful investment' in neighborhood growth.

title: Officer shooting sparks efforts to tighten parole policy
description: After an officer was injured in Sioux Falls by a parolee, law enforcement leaders and the governor are asking for a tighter leash on parole regulations.

title: Company behind mining project near Pe' Sla withdraws plan
description: Opponents of a mining operation near the Pe’ Sla site in the Black Hills says the company behind project is backing off the plan.

title: Missing infant found dead in Rapid City; Father facing murder charge
description: After a short missing and endangered person’s advisory, a missing baby was found dead in Rapid City. Melinko Ivan Bagola was nine weeks old.

title: Sanford Health announces merger with Minnesota-based North Memorial Health
description: Officials with Sanford Health say they’ve signed an agreement to merge with North Memorial Health, a nonprofit health care provider based in the Twin Cities.

title: Xcel Energy settles for reduced rate increase in South Dakota
description: State regulators are approving Xcel Energy’s request for a rate hike, though it’s a smaller increase than the provider asked for.

title: As anti-panhandling campaign returns to Sioux Falls, so does criticism
description: A campaign to prevent panhandling in Sioux Falls is again facing pushback. A small protest in the city continues the conversation about the campaign.

title: Postpartum research project combines personal experience with poetry and art
description: A researcher at South Dakota State University hopes a forthcoming project can help doctors and patients navigate the feelings that come after giving birth through a project driven by her own lived experience.

title: Tourism up in Glacial Lakes and prairies area
description: As one of the state’s largest industries, communities across South Dakota are getting their slice of the tourism pie. For Brookings, that means utilizing location and maximizing the infrastructure the community already has.

title: GFP commission approves reduced deer hunting licenses in some areas
description: A state commission seeks to reduce deer hunting license and tag numbers across the state due to growing struggles with the population.

title: Housing Authority unsure how rural program impacted by airport loans
description: Members of a state housing board are unsure the impacts of recently passed legislation on rural housing if demand for currently unused loans begins to rise.

title: Black Hills mining opponents look ahead after company backs off Pe' Sla project
description: With Pete Lien and Sons backing down in the standoff over mining at Pe’ Sla, a sacred Black Hills site, many are chalking this in the win column for opposition. Though many contend work is not yet finished.

title: SD wheat yields expected better than southern states, official says
description: While the national wheat crop is projected to be the smallest in decades, South Dakota’s crop isn’t expected to see large reductions.

title: Collegebound: Tips for navigating dual credit and AP classes in South Dakota
description: Teacher Talk explores the many opportunities for South Dakota students to study at the college level long before they get to college.

title: A matter of scale: Lt. Gov. says corrections programming must build capacity for better outcomes
description: Lt. Gov. Tony Venhuizen provides an update from the governor's Corrections Rehabilitation Task Force.

title: South Dakota teaching apprenticeship cohorts to expand
description: The state Teacher Apprenticeship Pathway has both increased its cohort size and endowed about 50 new teachers. Advocates say in a state with a noted teacher shortage, it represents steps toward closing the gap for educators.

title: Planting season for major South Dakota crops underway in some areas
description: It's prime planting season for corn, soybean and other crops after a record-breaking harvest last year.

title: Fertilizer costs at forefront of farmers minds and U.S. Senate committee
description: Fertilizer costs are at the forefront of many farmers minds and at a recent U.S. Senate Committee this week.

title: How the screen you're looking at is affecting your health
description: Our modern screen-filled lives may have impacts on our health in unexpected ways. A new book explores the implications.

title: Attorney General expects execution in Piper case by end of 2026
description: With appeal options nearly exhausted, it appears likely the life of the state's lone death row inmate will end by way of lethal injection. The state Attorney General said that execution is likely by the end of this year.

title: Centerville man sentenced to life in prison for 2024 triple homicide
description: A Centerville man has been sentenced to life in prison after being convicted in a triple homicide.

title: SBA disaster loans available to some South Dakota counties affected by drought
description: The U.S. Small Business Administration said it’s making low-interest disaster loans available to some small businesses in South Dakota impacted by drought.

title: Investigating missing persons cases in South Dakota
description: Attorney General Marty Jackley says there are over 100 missing persons cases in South Dakota, and more than half involve Native individuals. Jackley talks about ways the state is trying to improve how these cases are investigated.
